About

Builder.
Tinkerer.
Problem-solver.

I'm a sophomore ECE student at Cornell with a thing for hard problems — whether that's deploying real-time object detectors on embedded chips, designing custom PCBs, or benchmarking quantum ML models against classical baselines.

I like work that exists in the physical world. Code that drives a car, a weld scanner that actually ships to a factory floor, a circuit board I designed myself.

PythonC++ VerilogJava AssemblyPyTorch TensorFlowYOLO AltiumRaspberryPi JetsonPennyLane LAMMPSFusion 360
Experience

Cornell Electric Vehicles

Project Team · Ithaca, NY

Projects

Things I've built

SLAM for Self-Driving EV

Custom Simultaneous Localization and Mapping for Cornell Electric Vehicles' autonomous perception stack. Includes custom Altium PCBs and RP2040 embedded control.

SLAMROS2AltiumC++Embedded

Polymer Property Prediction

Graph Neural Networks in PyTorch Geometric to predict polymer properties. Used LAMMPS for molecular dynamics simulation. ~95% accuracy with pretrained transformers.

GNNPyTorchLAMMPSML

X-Ray Pneumonia Detector

YOLOv3 pipeline to localize pneumonia in chest X-rays. 53 mAP, with image augmentation and F1/confusion matrix evaluation.

YOLOv3Computer VisionPython

BMS Weld Fault Detector

YOLOv8 weld inspection app deployed in Matter Motor's production line, cutting in-line checking time by 60%. Now part of a filed patent.

YOLOv8ProductionCV
Research

Academic work

Quantum Machine Learning

Mentor: Prof. Nagendra Gajjar · Nirma University
Jun 2025 – Present

Built and benchmarked QML models in PennyLane. Found 2–10x slower runtimes vs classical due to simulation overhead, and ~10–20% accuracy drops across regression, image, and video tasks.

Sodium-Ion Battery Feasibility

Mentor: Stepan Ozerov, PhD · Purdue University
Aug 2024 – Jan 2025

Analyzed economic and electrochemical shortfalls of lithium-ion vs sodium-ion alternatives. Evaluated Na-ion viability for grid storage, EVs, and consumer electronics.
